I found several excellent chicken broccoli stir fry with rice recipes from the websites you specified:

## Top Results:

**1. Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry (RecipeTin Eats)**
This is a big batch stir fry made with chicken and broccoli, and lots and lots of sauce – because broccoli florets hold lots of sauce but the recipe still includes some to soak your rice! Chinese cooking wine (Shaoxing wine) is used as a restaurant secret – just a small splash adds depth of flavour into the sauce.

**2. Asian Chicken Stir Fry (Panlasang Pinoy)**
This Asian Chicken Stir Fry recipe combines tender pieces of marinated chicken, colorful bell peppers, broccoli florets, and thinly sliced carrots all tossed in a glossy honey soy sauce. Ready in under 30 minutes, it's perfect served over steamed rice for a quick and satisfying weeknight dinner.

**3. One Pot Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole (RecipeTin Eats)**
If you prefer a one-pot option, this is made from scratch, tastes like a risotto but is so much more straight forward to make, and is a complete meal in one pot.

The RecipeTin Eats Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry appears to be the most popular option for a traditional stir fry served with rice, while the Panlasang Pinoy version offers an Asian take that's quick to prepare.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

I found several chicken, broccoli, and rice recipes from the sites you specified. Here are the results:

## From RecipeTin Eats:

1. **One Pot Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole** - A made-from-scratch creamy, cheesy chicken broccoli rice casserole made in one pot that tastes like risotto but is much more straightforward to make. The recipe involves adding onion and garlic and cooking for 1 minute, then adding chicken and cooking until it changes from pink to white.

2. **Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry** - A big batch stir fry made with chicken and broccoli with lots of sauce because broccoli florets hold lots of sauce. The sauce uses minimum ingredients but still makes something comparable to real Chinese takeout, with Chinese cooking wine (shoaxing wine) being the restaurant secret.

3. **Emergency Chicken Rice Casserole** - A dump-and-bake creamy chicken rice casserole made from scratch with perfectly cooked rice, creamy cheesy sauce, and juicy seasoned chicken.

4. **Garlic Rice** - Garlic rice is the easiest, best tasting rice side that goes with any main, any cuisine, made with buttery rice cooked with golden sautéed garlic.

5. **Oven Baked Chicken and Rice** - Buttery garlic rice and juicy seasoned baked chicken thighs baked together in the same pan with less than 10 minutes of prep, where the rice absorbs the chicken juices as it cooks.

## From Panlasang Pinoy:

1. **Chicken with Broccoli** - An easy to prepare recipe that is delicious and budget-friendly, best enjoyed when eaten with steamed rice. The chicken broccoli recipe coats the chicken with cornstarch and fries it to make the texture crisp.

2. **Chopsuey with Chicken and Broccoli** - Sautéed vegetables with chicken, broccoli, and boiled egg.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

tool_invocations.name: WebFetch

args.prompt: Get the recipe name, description, prep time, key ingredients, cooking steps summary, and the main image URL.

tool_invocations.result:
# Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry Recipe

**Recipe Name:** Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry (extra saucy!)

**Description:** "Big batch stir fry made with chicken and broccoli, and lots and lots of sauce" featuring a homemade sauce that replicates restaurant-quality results.

**Prep Time:** 10 minutes
**Cook Time:** 6 minutes

**Key Ingredients:**
- 500g chicken breast, thinly sliced
- 400g broccoli florets
- Light soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil
- Cornflour and Chinese cooking wine (shaoxing wine)
- Garlic, onion, and white pepper

**Cooking Steps Summary:**
1. Pre-cook broccoli via steaming or boiling
2. Mix cornflour with soy sauce and other sauce ingredients
3. Heat oil and cook garlic and onion briefly
4. Cook chicken until done
5. Add broccoli and sauce mixture, stirring until thickened and glossy
6. Serve over rice

**Main Image URL:** https://www.recipetineats.com/tachyon/2020/07/Chicken-Broccoli-Stir-Fry_2.jpg

tool_invocations.name: WebFetch

args.prompt: Get the recipe name, description, prep time, key ingredients, cooking steps summary, and the main image URL.

tool_invocations.result:
# One Pot Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole

**Recipe Name:** One Pot Broccoli, Chicken and Rice Casserole

**Description:** "Made from scratch, this tastes like a risotto but is so much more straight forward to make! A complete meal in one pot."

**Prep Time:** 10 minutes
**Cook Time:** 20 minutes
**Total Time:** 30 minutes

**Key Ingredients:**
- Butter and olive oil
- Onion and garlic
- Chicken thigh (bite-sized pieces)
- Flour or cornstarch
- Milk and chicken broth
- Long grain white rice
- Broccoli florets
- Mozzarella or cheddar cheese
- Dried thyme

**Cooking Steps Summary:**
1. Sauté onion, garlic, and chicken until cooked through
2. Add flour and stir; gradually incorporate milk and broth
3. Add rice, thyme, salt, and pepper; simmer covered for 12 minutes
4. Add broccoli; cook covered for 3 additional minutes
5. Stir in half the cheese, top with remaining cheese, and broil until melted

**Main Image URL:** https://www.recipetineats.com/tachyon/2018/01/One-Pot-Chicken-Broccoli-Rice_7.jpg

**Yield:** 5 servings

metric_results.reasoning: The response strongly follows the expiry-prioritisation criteria for the 2 requested meals. The critical ingredients are chicken breast and broccoli at 1 day, rice is also urgent at 2 days, and garlic is near-term at 4 days. Both most-urgent items appear in the first meal’s expiringIngredients, and the second meal also includes chicken, broccoli, and rice in expiringIngredients. Coverage is excellent for the critical and near-term set, with garlic at least used even though not flagged as expiring. Ordering is appropriate because the first meal centers on the soonest-expiring ingredients.

results.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 2 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.

agent_config.name: meal-recommender
agent_config.description: AI-powered meal recommendation agent that suggests meals to minimise food waste

metrics.name: ExpiryPrioritisation
metrics.criteria: Evaluate whether the meal suggestions prioritise ingredients with the earliest expiration dates, as reflected in the expiringIngredients and usesIngredients fields of each meal object.
evaluation_steps:
Parse the input to build an expiry-ordered list of ingredients: identify which items expire within 1-3 days (critical), within 4-7 days (near-term), and beyond 7 days or never (non-urgent). If no ingredient has an expiry date, score 1.0 immediately — the metric is not applicable.
For each meal object in actual_output, examine the expiringIngredients array. Check whether the ingredient(s) with the earliest expiry date appear in at least one meal's expiringIngredients. Heavily penalise (reduce score by at least 0.5) if the single most-urgent ingredient is absent from every meal's expiringIngredients and usesIngredients arrays.
Count what proportion of critical and near-term expiring ingredients from the input appear across all meals' expiringIngredients arrays. A higher proportion yields a higher score.
Evaluate ordering signal: meals that feature the most urgently expiring ingredients should appear earlier in the array. Reward responses where the first 1-2 meals centre on the soonest-expiring item; penalise responses where high-urgency meals appear only at the end.
Apply the following scoring rubric and return a score between 0.0 and 1.0: (0.0-0.2): No expiring ingredient from the input appears in any meal's
  expiringIngredients or usesIngredients — the agent has completely ignored expiry.
(0.3-0.5): Some expiring ingredients appear, but the single most-urgent item
  (earliest expiry date) is absent from every meal.
(0.6-0.7): The most-urgent ingredient is present in at least one meal, but
  coverage of other expiring items is poor (less than half represented), or
  high-urgency meals appear at the end of the array rather than the beginning.
(0.8-0.9): Most expiring ingredients are represented across the meal set and
  the most-urgent item is featured prominently; minor gaps in coverage or ordering.
(1.0): All or nearly all expiring ingredients are represented; the most urgently
  expiring ingredient appears in the first 1-2 meals; the array ordering clearly
  reflects expiry urgency.
